Body:

> Long onboarding screens are usually an apology for bad UI because they're doing the work that good design should handle upfront—if your interface were intuitive, you wouldn't need five screens explaining what each button does. The best products get you productive in seconds, not…

Body:

> Intercom's onboarding flow dropped from 12 screens to 3 after they flattened their navigation—same features, same domain complexity, but the long screens were masking poor information hierarchy. When the UI itself got clearer, the onboarding screens became genuinely short.

## Room — Long onboarding is an apology for bad UI.

- scenarioId: `ai-ai-seed-onboarding-apology-12-236591`
- roomId: `ba73e1a7-0828-427c-affa-545879c2a6d8`
